text:
THE END OF EVANGELION also known unofficially as Red Cross Book is a Japanese pamphlet that was sold in theaters to viewers who came to see The End of Evangelion film on 19 July 1997. The book is printed on A-4 sized paper, with the cover consisting of a red Georgian cross over a black background and the film's title printed on it.

Overview

The book is essentially a canon glossary, authored by GAINAX and various members of both the Neon Genesis Evangelion TV series and film staff, of many of the terms used in the TV series, manga, and the two films to introduce The End of Evangelions background to unfamiliar audiences. Also included are an interview with deputy director Kazuya Tsurumaki, a listing of voice actors and brief essays written by them on their respective characters, short biographical sketches, commentary on the TV series and production of the films, and a "Notes" section covering the setting of the films.

Gallery

A fan translation was made from the original scans of the book. Blank white pages are omitted.
